# Web3 Staking
> An independent, non-custodial dashboard for understanding and comparing wallet-based staking routes across proof-of-stake networks.

## What is Web3 Staking
Web3 staking commits crypto assets to a proof-of-stake network or staking protocol in exchange for protocol-defined rewards. Native staking helps validators secure consensus; pooled and liquid staking make participation easier or provide a transferable receipt token. Non-custodial staking keeps private keys with the user, although assets may become bonded or controlled by smart-contract rules. It differs from lending and yield farming, which generate yield without directly securing network consensus.

## How it works
1. Connect or select a wallet and verify the intended asset and network.
2. Choose native delegation, solo validation, pooled staking, or liquid staking and review fees, validator terms, activation, and exit rules.
3. Approve the exact transaction in the wallet; the dashboard never needs the seed phrase or private key.
4. The network or protocol records the stake, assigns validator weight, and calculates rewards and penalties under its rules.
5. To exit, submit an undelegation or redemption transaction and wait for any protocol queue, cooldown, or unbonding period.

## Key facts
- Native staking applies to proof-of-stake assets such as ETH, SOL, and ADA.
- Ethereum Mainnet uses chain ID `1`; Solana’s production cluster is `mainnet-beta`.
- Common models are solo validation, delegated staking, staking-as-a-service, pooled staking, and liquid staking.
- Non-custodial means the user retains wallet keys; it does not mean staked assets remain immediately transferable.
- Liquid staking tokens represent a staking position and add smart-contract, liquidity, and price-deviation risk.
- EVM liquid-staking receipts are often ERC-20 tokens, but no universal staking-token standard exists.
- Validators can earn protocol rewards and may incur penalties or slashing under network-specific rules.
- USDT does not secure proof-of-stake consensus; “USDT staking” routes are normally DeFi lending, liquidity, or incentive programs.
- Activation and withdrawal timing are chain-specific and can include epochs, queues, cooldowns, or unbonding periods.

## Common questions
### Is Web3 staking safe?
Staking has validator, slashing, smart-contract, phishing, liquidity, and token-price risks. Non-custody removes direct control of private keys by the dashboard but does not remove protocol risk.

### Who has custody?
The user controls the wallet and signs every transaction. Delegated or deposited assets remain subject to the selected network or contract’s withdrawal rules.

### What fees apply?
Costs can include network transaction fees, validator commission, protocol fees, and liquid-token swap costs.

### How long does staking take?
Signing is immediate, but confirmation, activation, rewards, and unstaking depend on the network and current queues.

### Which assets and chains are supported?
Use the dashboard’s current listings. Native examples include ETH on Ethereum, SOL on Solana, and ADA on Cardano; stablecoin routes are DeFi yield rather than native staking.

### How do I start?
Use the official dashboard, select the correct network, compare validator and exit terms, verify the destination, and approve only the expected wallet transaction.

### What are the main risks?
Key risks are slashing, downtime penalties, malicious contracts, operator failure, receipt-token depegging, illiquidity, changing rewards, and signing on the wrong network.

## App
- Dashboard: https://web3-staking-page.github.io/
- App: https://web3-staking-page.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Ethereum Staking: https://ethereum.org/staking/
Ethereum Staking Launchpad: https://launchpad.ethereum.org/
Ethereum Rewards and Penalties: https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/consensus-mechanisms/pos/rewards-and-penalties/
Solana Staking: https://solana.com/staking
Cardano Delegation: https://docs.cardano.org/about-cardano/learn/delegation
Cosmos SDK Staking Module: https://docs.cosmos.network/sdk/v0.50/build/modules/staking/README

## Data & references
Proof of Stake: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proof_of_stake
Ethereum Validator Explorer: https://beaconcha.in/
Solana Explorer: https://solscan.io/
DeFiLlama Yields: https://defillama.com/yields
CoinGecko Liquid Staking Tokens: https://www.coingecko.com/en/categories/liquid-staking-tokens
CAIP-2 Blockchain IDs: https://standards.chainagnostic.org/CAIPs/caip-2
